title: Blind Date

jokes.text: Still nothing. You know, an actor doesn't like to hear that but Angela... nobody called me... She gives me other people's messages.
jokes.setup: Bobby explains how most operators just say 'still nothing' to actors waiting for calls
jokes.punchline: Angela gives him other people's messages instead, showing both her kindness and his desperation
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Bobby's actor desperation and Angela's sweet nature while being genuinely clever

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: So most operators just say 'still nothing' when actors call in waiting for work.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: But I give them other people's messages instead.

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: It's actually very kind of her.

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: And very desperate of me.

jokes.text: Sometimes she gives me Gene Shalit's messages. Yeah. Gene and me we use the same service, you know. A lot of stars call him. You know who called him yesterday? Who? Erma Bombeck.
jokes.setup: Bobby builds up anticipation about a big star calling Gene Shalit
jokes.punchline: It's Erma Bombeck, a humor columnist, not exactly a glamorous celebrity
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and character work showing Bobby's skewed perspective on fame

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: You won't believe who just called Gene Shalit!

dialogue.speaker: Gene
dialogue.line: Who?

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: Erma Bombeck!

dialogue.speaker: Gene
dialogue.line: Erma Bombeck?

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: Yeah, the humor columnist.

jokes.text: That's longer than most of your fights last.
jokes.setup: Tony mentions talking to Angela for five minutes about his boxing career
jokes.punchline: Alex points out this is longer than Tony's actual fights
jokes.explanation: Sharp, economical roast of Tony's boxing skills with perfect timing

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Wait, you talked to Angela about your boxing career for five minutes? That's longer than any of your actual fights.

jokes.text: She might turn out to be a dog.
jokes.setup: Bobby explains why he never asked Angela out despite great phone conversations
jokes.punchline: Crude but honest assessment that sets up the episode's theme
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's central conflict but relies on crude language rather than wit

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: Look, we had great conversations on the phone, but I could never ask her out. Every time I thought about it, I'd remember what she looks like.

jokes.text: Sure, I would. If I was real drunk or something.
jokes.setup: Elaine challenges Bobby about only dating good-looking women
jokes.punchline: Bobby admits he'd date an unattractive woman only if intoxicated
jokes.explanation: Honest character moment but predictable and somewhat crude

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Bobby, you only date beautiful women. What if you met someone who wasn't conventionally attractive?

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: Well, I'd have to be drunk.

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Drunk?

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: Yeah, very drunk. I mean, I'd need several drinks before I could even consider it.

jokes.text: How popular are short, pudgy, ugly men?
jokes.setup: Elaine criticizes men for being shallow about looks
jokes.punchline: Bobby points out women are equally shallow, using self-deprecating description
jokes.explanation: Fair point about double standards but not particularly clever execution

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Men are so shallow. You're all just focused on looks.

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: Women are just as shallow. I mean, look at me - I'm short, I'm bald, I drive a cab. You think any woman would give me a second look?

jokes.text: I hope someone slams a door on your nose and you sneeze and your head explodes.
jokes.setup: Bobby asks Louie how popular short, pudgy, ugly men are
jokes.punchline: Louie responds with elaborate, cartoonish threat instead of answering
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Louie's vindictive creativity and provides memorable, quotable insult

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: How popular are short, pudgy, ugly men?

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: You want to know how popular short, pudgy, ugly men are? I'll tell you how popular they are. I'm gonna take you down to the docks, I'm gonna tie you to an anchor, and I'm gonna throw you in the river. Then I'm gonna wait for the tide to come in, and when it does, I'm gonna pull you out and feed you to the sharks. And you know what the sharks are gonna do? They're gonna spit you out!

jokes.text: Anything for Shalit?
jokes.setup: Bobby calls for his messages
jokes.punchline: He also checks for Gene Shalit's messages, following up on earlier joke
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that reinforces Bobby's delusions about celebrity status

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: Any messages for me?

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Yeah, you got a call from Gene Shalit.

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: Gene Shalit? What did he want?

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: He didn't say. But he also left a message for you to check HIS messages.

jokes.text: Yeah, I remember your voice, too even though I knew it was you. I mean, even if I didn't know it was you and I heard your voice, I would know it was you. You want to go out with me?
jokes.setup: Alex gets nervous talking to Angela on the phone
jokes.punchline: His rambling, nonsensical attempt at charm followed by abrupt date request
jokes.explanation: Realistic nervous rambling that shows Alex's character vulnerability

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Angela? Hi! It's Alex. Yeah, from the cab company. The yellow cabs. Well, they're not always yellow, some of them are a little more of a... ochre situation, but... anyway, I was thinking about you today. Actually, I was thinking about you yesterday too. And the day before. Not in a creepy way! More of a... mathematical way. Like, statistically speaking, you occupy a significant portion of my thoughts, which when you divide it by the total hours in a day, comes to... I'm not good with math. Do you like math? You seem like you'd be good at math. Or ballet. Do you do ballet? Anyway, would you maybe want to go out with me sometime?

jokes.text: Smooth, Alex.
jokes.setup: Alex's awkward phone conversation with Angela
jokes.punchline: Bobby's sarcastic commentary on Alex's lack of smoothness
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective comedic beat that punctuates Alex's awkwardness

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: Hey Alex, you're really smooth with the ladies, aren't you? That's why Angela hung up on you.

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: You know, most guys can at least get through a whole sentence without sounding like they're reading a ransom note.

jokes.text: Not smackers. Bucks.
jokes.setup: Someone says '2,000 smackers' about the found money
jokes.punchline: Louie corrects the slang with pedantic precision
jokes.explanation: Minor character beat showing Louie's nitpicking nature

dialogue.speaker: Character
dialogue.line: 2,000 smackers.

dialogue.speaker: Louie De Palma
dialogue.line: Two thousand dollars. 'Smackers' is an informal colloquialism. The correct term is dollars.

jokes.text: I didn't say I lost it. I just said I was looking for it. I've been looking for 2,000 bucks all my life.
jokes.setup: Someone questions Louie about losing 2,000 bucks
jokes.punchline: Louie clarifies he's been looking for money his whole life, not that he lost it
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that reveals Louie's opportunistic character

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: I didn't lose two thousand bucks. I've been looking for money my whole life!

jokes.text: Tell her I'll get back to her.
jokes.setup: Louie is told his mother is calling because someone's breaking into her apartment
jokes.punchline: Louie treats this emergency like a routine business call
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Louie's callousness toward family emergencies

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Ma, I'm gonna have to call you back. I got a cab waiting.

jokes.text: A priest who collected it for underprivileged crippled children. Or a nun on her way to send it to starving natives.
jokes.setup: The guys suggest only bad people carry 2,000 in cash
jokes.punchline: Elaine offers increasingly virtuous alternatives to shame their cynicism
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that shows Elaine's moral compass versus the guys' cynicism

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: What if I'm carrying it to donate to charity?

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: What if I'm carrying it to help a friend in need?

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: What if I'm carrying it because I'm a teacher and I'm going to use it for school supplies for underprivileged children?

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: What if I'm carrying it because I just found out I have a terminal illness and I want to spend my last days doing good deeds?

jokes.text: Or a hooker.
jokes.setup: Elaine lists virtuous people who might carry cash
jokes.punchline: Tony adds the opposite extreme, completely missing Elaine's point
jokes.explanation: Perfect character beat showing Tony's obliviousness to Elaine's sarcasm

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Well, you know, virtuous people carry cash. Like priests, nuns, Boy Scouts...

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Yeah, and muggers, rapists, and murderers!

jokes.text: It killed my sister-in-law.
jokes.setup: Tony recommends Mario's restaurant but warns against the clams oreganata with eggplant
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that the dish killed his sister-in-law
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with Tony's characteristic casual tone

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: You go to Mario's, you get the clams oreganata with eggplant? Don't do it.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: That's what killed my sister-in-law.

jokes.text: You could have said you were looking for another apartment. No. I was looking for this one, 3-D. It fell off when I knocked on... door.
jokes.setup: Angela suggests Alex could lie about why he's there
jokes.punchline: Alex's honest but awkward explanation about the apartment number falling off
jokes.explanation: Realistic awkwardness that builds the uncomfortable dynamic

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: You could just lie about why you're here.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: I can't lie. I'm honest. See, the thing is, I was looking for apartment 3B, but the '3' fell off the door, so I thought it said 'B', and I didn't know where 'B' was, so I tried all the apartments until I found one that looked like it might be 'B'.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: That's worse than the truth.

jokes.text: Well, my admirers will just have to wander the halls looking for me.
jokes.setup: Discussion about the apartment number falling off
jokes.punchline: Angela sarcastically suggests she has admirers who would be searching for her
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating humor that shows Angela's defensive wit

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Well, maybe it fell off because I have so many admirers searching for me.

jokes.text: You got guts.
jokes.setup: Alex says he wants to come into her apartment
jokes.punchline: Angela sarcastically compliments his 'courage' for staying
jokes.explanation: Quick character beat showing Angela's bitter sense of humor

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: I want to come into your apartment.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Oh, how courageous of you to stay.

jokes.text: Most guys drop it ten seconds after they see me. You can stop now. You've got the record.
jokes.setup: Angela comments on Alex's smile
jokes.punchline: She reveals most guys stop smiling quickly after seeing her, then congratulates Alex for lasting longest
jokes.explanation: Painfully honest and funny self-awareness that establishes the episode's emotional stakes

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: You know, most guys stop smiling after they see me.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: But you've been smiling the longest.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Congratulations.

jokes.text: To call your mother. Maybe you'll be lucky and she'll be sick and you'll have to run over and see her.
jokes.setup: Angela offers Alex the phone when he seems uncomfortable
jokes.punchline: She suggests he call his mother hoping for an emergency escape
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Angela's expectation of rejection

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Do you want to call your mother?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Why would I want to do that?

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: I don't know, maybe she'll say something that'll get you out of here.

jokes.text: You must know a really dark bar where no one will see us.
jokes.setup: Alex suggests getting drinks somewhere else
jokes.punchline: Angela assumes he wants to hide their date from public view
jokes.explanation: Captures Angela's defensive assumptions about how men see her

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: You want to go somewhere else? What, you don't want to be seen with me in public?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: No, no, that's not it at all.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Oh, so you're ashamed of me.

jokes.text: Sure, why take any chance on anyone seeing you with me.
jokes.setup: Alex suggests they could have drinks at her place
jokes.punchline: Angela interprets this as him wanting to hide her
jokes.explanation: Continues the pattern of Angela's defensive misinterpretations

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: We could have drinks at your place.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Oh, so you want to hide me?

jokes.text: As you can see, I eat anything.
jokes.setup: Alex asks if she wants to go for Italian food
jokes.punchline: Angela's self-deprecating comment about her eating habits and appearance
jokes.explanation: Painful self-awareness that builds sympathy while maintaining humor

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Italian food? That's what I need — to look like a meatball.

jokes.text: I was just trying to make it easy on you.
jokes.setup: Alex insists they don't have to go out if she doesn't want to
jokes.punchline: Angela assumes he's looking for an easy way to avoid being seen with her
jokes.explanation: Reinforces Angela's pattern of defensive misreading of kindness

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: We don't have to go out if you don't want to.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Oh, I see what this is. You're looking for an easy way out of being seen with me in public.

jokes.text: Fat chance.
jokes.setup: Alex insists dinner will be fun
jokes.punchline: Angela's response uses 'fat' in the idiom, creating unintended self-reference
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels given Angela's character

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Dinner will be fun, Angela. I promise.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Alex, the last time you promised me fun, I had such a fat chance of enjoying myself.

jokes.text: Do you like clams oreganata with eggplant?
jokes.setup: Alex trying to make conversation about food
jokes.punchline: He's asking about the dish that killed Tony's sister-in-law
jokes.explanation: Dark callback that adds ominous undertone to an innocent question

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: So what was that dish that killed Tony's sister-in-law?

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Lasagna.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: No, I mean what was it called?

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: It was called lasagna.

jokes.text: Rich people probably like them. Rich people like anything that's small that tastes weird.
jokes.setup: Discussion about never meeting anyone who likes anchovies
jokes.punchline: Angela's theory about rich people's food preferences
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor that shows Angela's wit beneath the defensiveness

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: You know, I have a theory about rich people. They eat things like anchovies and caviar not because they like them, but because they're expensive and nobody else can afford them. It's just a way to show off.

jokes.text: The management requested it.
jokes.setup: Alex asks why she still has her coat on
jokes.punchline: Angela claims the restaurant asked her to keep it on
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating humor that's both funny and genuinely sad

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Why do you still have your coat on?

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: The restaurant asked me to keep it on.

jokes.text: I once went out with a girl who pulled a knife on me, robbed me and threw me out of her car on the New Jersey turnpike. I have to think about that now as a medium date.
jokes.setup: Angela asks if Alex isn't having a good time
jokes.punchline: Alex compares this date to an extremely traumatic previous experience, calling it 'medium'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and perspective humor that's both absurd and relatable

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Aren't you having a good time?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Oh, it's medium.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Medium?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Yeah. I went out with a girl once who made me sit in the car while she went into a store. She was gone for three hours. When she came back, she didn't even apologize. She just said, 'I got distracted.' This is better than that, so I'd call it medium.

jokes.text: Am I what you pictured, Bobby? Yeah, more or less. More.
jokes.setup: Angela asks Bobby if she matches his mental picture
jokes.punchline: Bobby's diplomatic 'more or less' followed by clarifying 'More'
jokes.explanation: Well-structured joke showing Bobby's tactless honesty

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Do I match your mental picture of me?

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: More or less.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: More or less? What does that mean?

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: More.

jokes.text: Maybe Alex will give you her number. You could call her up for tomorrow night. Oh, no. Thank you very much.
jokes.setup: Bobby suggests Latka might like Angela and could ask her out
jokes.punchline: Latka's polite but firm rejection
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable moment that highlights the cruelty of the situation

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: No, no, Bobby. I cannot ask Angela out.

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: Why not? You like her, don't you?

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: Yes, but is not appropriate. She is too good for me.

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: That's not true. Just ask her out.

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: No, Bobby. In my country, a man does not pursue a woman so forward. He must show respect.

jokes.text: 'cause nobody trusts nobody. Except you. Everybody trusts you.
jokes.setup: The gang explains why they need Alex to hold the money
jokes.punchline: They point out Alex is the only trustworthy one among them
jokes.explanation: Character-based humor that reveals group dynamics

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: We need you to hold the money, Alex.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: You're the only one we can trust.

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: Yeah, I'd spend it.

dialogue.speaker: Danny
dialogue.line: I'd lose it.

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: I'd give it away.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: And I'd steal it.

jokes.text: Everything go all right in the ladies' room?
jokes.setup: Angela returns from the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Tony's inappropriately personal question about her bathroom experience
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable humor showing Tony's social awkwardness

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: So, did you take care of everything?

jokes.text: One of the guys told a joke.
jokes.setup: Angela accuses the guys of laughing at her
jokes.punchline: Alex's obvious lie about someone telling a joke
jokes.explanation: Weak lie that highlights Alex's discomfort with the situation

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: You guys are laughing at me!

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: No, no, we're not laughing at you. Bobby just told us the funniest joke about a priest, a rabbi, and a penguin.

jokes.text: You could have held out. I was almost ready to believe you.
jokes.setup: Alex admits the guys were laughing at Angela
jokes.punchline: Angela sarcastically notes he gave up the lie too easily
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that shows Angela's wit and resignation

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: You know, you gave up that lie awfully fast. Most men would've stuck with it.

jokes.text: I'm one of the few women in New York who's safe on the streets.
jokes.setup: Angela insists she doesn't need an escort home
jokes.punchline: Dark self-deprecating humor about her appearance being protection from crime
jokes.explanation: Clever, dark humor that's both funny and genuinely sad

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: I don't need an escort home. Who's going to bother me? I've got a face that could stop traffic. Not because I'm beautiful — because drivers would swerve to avoid it.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Besides, no mugger would touch me. They'd be afraid I'd sue them for emotional damages after seeing me in the mirror.

jokes.text: They give you a little dinner, they think they own you.
jokes.setup: Alex insists on escorting her home
jokes.punchline: Angela's cynical take on men's expectations after buying dinner
jokes.explanation: Bitter but relatable observation about dating dynamics

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Oh, so you buy a guy dinner and suddenly you owe him your body? That's the deal?

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: For a $12.95 meatloaf special, I'm supposed to let him see me in my slip?

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: What if he'd taken me to McDonald's? Would I owe him a hand job in the parking lot?

jokes.text: Did you said foreigner?
jokes.setup: Louie calls Latka a 'dumb foreigner'
jokes.punchline: Latka's offended reaction with grammar that proves Louie's point
jokes.explanation: Character-specific humor that plays with Latka's language struggles

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: I am not dumb! I am very intelligent! I speak five languages!

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: You are the dumb one, Mr. De Palma. I have more brains in my little finger than you have in your whole head!

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: And another thing — I don't appreciate you calling me 'dumb foreigner.' It are very insulting!

jokes.text: You better smile when you do that, buddy.
jokes.setup: Latka makes a threatening gesture
jokes.punchline: Louie's tough guy response escalating the confrontation
jokes.explanation: Standard tough guy posturing that fits Louie's character

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: You wanna piece of me?

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Piece of you? I want the whole thing!

jokes.text: I accept your apology.
jokes.setup: Physical confrontation building between Louie and Latka
jokes.punchline: Latka suddenly defuses it by accepting an apology that wasn't offered
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection that shows Latka's unique conflict resolution style

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: You know what? I accept your apology.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: What apology? I didn't apologize!

dialogue.speaker: Latka
dialogue.line: I know. That's what I appreciate about you.

jokes.text: Hey, Alex, did you score?
jokes.setup: Alex returns from his disastrous date
jokes.punchline: Bobby's crudely inappropriate question about sexual success
jokes.explanation: Crass but character-appropriate question that highlights Bobby's insensitivity

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: So, did you get lucky?

jokes.text: Looking good, per usual...
jokes.setup: Louie begins telling a story about his blind date
jokes.punchline: His delusional self-assessment of his appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Louie's complete lack of self-awareness

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: So I went on this blind date, right? The woman takes one look at me and says, 'You're Louie?' I say, 'Yeah, what's wrong?' She says, 'Nothing, I just expected someone... taller.' I say, 'I AM tall! I'm five-foot-ten!' She says, 'In what? Heels?'

jokes.text: I prefer my women 'H and H'... Homely and hot.
jokes.setup: Louie claims the beautiful girl wasn't his type
jokes.punchline: His crude categorization system for women
jokes.explanation: Crude but memorable Louie-ism that reveals his warped worldview

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: She wasn't my type.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: My type is: female, and willing.

jokes.text: You're really a poet, you know?
jokes.setup: Louie's crude 'H and H' comment
jokes.punchline: Elaine's sarcastic praise for his eloquence
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcastic reaction that provides necessary pushback to Louie

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Oh, that's beautiful, Louie. Really eloquent. You should write greeting cards.

jokes.text: I tried to take her blouse off. It's not my fault she feels sick so why shouldn't one of us have a good time, right?
jokes.setup: Louie's date claims to be sick with cholera
jokes.punchline: His selfish, inappropriate response to her illness
jokes.explanation: Appallingly in-character moment that shows Louie's complete lack of empathy

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Cholera? Are you kidding me? Do you know what this means? I paid forty bucks for dinner!

jokes.text: Every time I talk to you, Louie I feel grateful that I haven't eaten yet.
jokes.setup: Louie's disgusting story about his date
jokes.punchline: Elaine's perfectly crafted expression of revulsion
jokes.explanation: Clever and eloquent putdown that perfectly captures everyone's reaction to Louie

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Oh my God, that's disgusting!

jokes.text: Maybe Angela ate her.
jokes.setup: Alex wonders what happened to the witty girl from the phone
jokes.punchline: Elaine's cruel joke about Angela's weight
jokes.explanation: Mean-spirited joke that feels out of character for typically compassionate Elaine

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: You know, I just realized something. Angela's been so quiet lately. I think she's finally figured out that the more she eats, the wider she gets. It's like watching a time-lapse of a balloon being inflated.

jokes.text: I feel like I'm walking away from a car wreck with a person trapped inside.
jokes.setup: Alex explains why he feels compelled to see Angela again
jokes.punchline: Metaphor comparing Angela to a trapped accident victim
jokes.explanation: Powerful metaphor that captures both compassion and the episode's central theme

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: It's like when you pass an accident on the highway. You don't want to look, but you can't help yourself. You have to know if everyone's okay.

jokes.text: Yeah, he'll probably just blow it on rent or food or something.
jokes.setup: Discussion about giving all the found money to Latka
jokes.punchline: Tony's dismissive attitude toward basic necessities
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing the group's skewed priorities about money

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: What do you need food for? You're always eating.

jokes.text: You want to pop the veins in my head?
jokes.setup: The suggestion to give all the money to Latka
jokes.punchline: Louie's dramatic physical reaction to the idea
jokes.explanation: Typical Louie overreaction with colorful physical imagery

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: *clutches chest, staggers backward, eyes bulge*

jokes.text: What are you doing this time praying nobody will be home?
jokes.setup: Alex appears at Angela's door again
jokes.punchline: Angela's assumption that he's hoping to avoid her
jokes.explanation: Continues Angela's pattern of defensive assumptions

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Alex, what are you doing here again?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: I just wanted to stop by and say hello.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: You're hoping I won't be home, aren't you?

jokes.text: Good, so far it looks like I haven't been missing anything.
jokes.setup: Alex declares this their second date since Angela's never had one
jokes.punchline: Angela's dry assessment of dating based on their first experience
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery that shows Angela's wit beneath the pain

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Well, based on my first date, I'd say dating is a lot of sitting around waiting for someone to tell you what to do.

jokes.text: You mean falling in love?
jokes.setup: Alex wants to avoid the 'stupid stuff' from last night
jokes.punchline: Angela sarcastically suggests he means romantic feelings
jokes.explanation: Sharp sarcastic response that cuts to the emotional core

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: I just want to forget about all that stupid stuff from last night.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: You mean like having romantic feelings?

jokes.text: Now, feel free to jump in and stop me anytime.
jokes.setup: Angela describes herself as not beautiful, good-looking, or even plain
jokes.punchline: She invites Alex to contradict her self-deprecation
jokes.explanation: Painful honesty mixed with hope for contradiction

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: I'm not beautiful, I'm not good-looking, I'm not even plain.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Go ahead, contradict me.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: I couldn't even if I wanted to.

jokes.text: and dropped ten or 70 pounds.
jokes.setup: Angela talks about getting a makeover
jokes.punchline: The dramatic jump from 10 to 70 pounds in weight loss needed
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating escalation that's both funny and genuinely sad

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: I got a makeover today. The stylist said I needed to lose weight to really see the results.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: She said I need to lose 10 pounds. Then she looked at me again and said, 'Actually, make that 70.'

jokes.text: I haven't been romantically involved in eight years and I'm not about to break my streak right now.
jokes.setup: Angela asks about romantic attraction
jokes.punchline: Alex frames his loneliness as an achievement to maintain
jokes.explanation: Clever reframing of failure as success that reveals Alex's defense mechanisms

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Are you attracted to me?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Well, sure, but I'm working very hard at not doing anything about it.

jokes.text: I can't believe you came all the way back here just because you forgot to give me the 'let's be friends' speech.
jokes.setup: Alex explains he wants to be friends
jokes.punchline: Angela recognizes this as a standard rejection formula
jokes.explanation: Smart observation about dating clichés that shows Angela's experience with rejection

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: I think you're a wonderful person, Angela. I'd like us to be friends.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Oh, I know that line. That's the 'Let's be friends' brush-off. Next you'll tell me it's not me, it's you.

jokes.text: Hey, Angela, I think we had a normal moment there.
jokes.setup: After establishing their friendship ground rules
jokes.punchline: Alex's recognition that they finally had an unstrained interaction
jokes.explanation: Sweet observation that marks the turning point in their relationship

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: You know what? This is great. We finally had a conversation where neither of us was uncomfortable.

jokes.text: No. A half an hour.
jokes.setup: Alex asks how long it's been since Angela cried
jokes.punchline: She reveals it was only 30 minutes ago
jokes.explanation: Perfectly timed emotional honesty that's both heartbreaking and darkly funny

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: How long has it been since you cried?

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Thirty minutes ago.

tmdb_overview: Alex finds himself inexplicably attracted to an overweight and abrasive blind date.
insight: Early Taxi trades laugh volume for awkward chemistry—60 jokes land unevenly across two leads' mismatched date.
